mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly increasing present value of future corporate cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.13% as of 2019-08-01 reflects recent Fed rate cuts
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% as of 2019-08-01 remains historically low
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a decline in housing starts (HOUST) over the preceding 2 months indicates weakening aggregate demand that foreshadows lower corporate revenues and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1191k units (2019-07-01) shows recent softness
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or joint Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over 3 months signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, indicating lower near-term corporate profitability and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.18 (2019-07-01) reflects recent manufacturing softness
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (mean return > 0 with t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) decelerates below 100k new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, signaling a shift toward labor market slack that will eventually pressure wage growth and corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100k while UNRATE unchanged or rising
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following the interaction trigger are positive in > 55% of instances, or joint Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, the real policy rate becomes negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through lower real discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 256.3 (2019-08-01) and FEDFUNDS at 2.13% create accommodative real rate
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during accommodative real rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate over two consecutive quarters, signaling an economy approaching stall-speed that will compress corporate earnings multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as recession risk premiums increase.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2% for 2 consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19023.022 billion (2019-04-01 vintage) shows recent moderation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following stall-speed GDP signals are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 12-month trend while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth remains above 150k per month, signaling robust supply-side expansion and tight labor markets that support corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 12-month trend AND PAYEMS monthly growth > 150k
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151541k (2019-08-01) near threshold, INDPRO at 109.18 shows supply resilience
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with strong supply and labor dynamics
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during joint supply-labor strength periods are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% with t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
